channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from implementing Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal judge expanded his ruling temporarily blocking the Trump administration from invoking the Alien Enemies Act of 1798 on Saturday preventing the removal of Venezuelan undocumented immigrants presumed to be members of the Tren de Aragua gang. The order issued by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States. U.S. District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to gut staff at Voice of America and other US-funded media organizations
item.description: President Donald Trump is ordering his administration to gut Voice of America and other pro-democracy media organizations run by the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Democrats to hold 'Day of Action' to push back against GOP-backed spending bill
item.description: House Democrats will hold a "Day of Action" across the country on Tuesday to push back against the GOP-backed spending bill to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump undoes stack of 'harmful' Biden executive orders, shrinks multiple agencies
item.description: The White House on Friday said President Donald Trump took more actions to downsize the federal government through several executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican legislation seeks to ban Chinese nationals from studying in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of House Republicans on Friday put forward legislation seeking to prevent Chinese students from studying in American schools, as some U.S. lawmakers are targeting China over national security concerns. Rep. Riley Moore, R-W.Va.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spending bill to avert shutdown successfully beats filibuster with hours until deadline
item.description: The Senate voted on a key procedural motion regarding the House-passed stopgap spending bill on Friday. If a measure is not passed by midnight, the government will enter a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian reporters grill Rubio over Trump's 51st state talk
item.description: Rubio said Trump’s stance on Canada joining the U.S. came following a dinner conversation with former Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: As 'match day' nears, med schools, lawmakers want to expand residencies to keep doctors in Nevada
item.description: On Friday, March 21 — just a week away — more than 40,000 medical students around the country will find out where they will be serving their residencies. About 300 of those graduates are from UNLV, UNR and Touro University, but there aren't enough residencies in the state to keep them here.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: No action taken after ethics panel deadlocks on complaint against Sen. Nicole Mitchell
item.description: An ethics panel deadlocked over a new set of complaints filed against Woodbury Sen. Nicole Mitchell. The ethics process is again on hold until after a felony case against her has concluded.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of fired federal workers must be offered reinstatement, a judge rules
item.description: Thousands of probationary federal employees fired by the Trump administration must be offered job reinstatement, a judge in San Francisco has ruled, because they were terminated unlawfully.
item.dc:creator: Chris Arnold and Emily Feng, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
